label: 正常文书 - 数学
essay_text: Growing up in Singapore, mathematics was not just a subject but a lens through which I understood the world. My first encounter with proof by induction left me in awe of how infinite truths could spring from finite foundations. At A-level, I achieved A* in Mathematics and Further Mathematics, and I have spent the past year self-studying real analysis through Rudin's Principles of Mathematical Analysis. Beyond the classroom, I participated in the Singapore Mathematical Olympiad, reaching the national round. I believe Cambridge's rigorous approach to pure mathematics will deepen my understanding of the structures that underlie computation, cryptography, and physics.

label: 正常文书 - 计算机
essay_text: The first time I wrote a sorting algorithm and watched it arrange a thousand numbers in milliseconds, I understood the power of abstraction. Since then, I have built three web applications, contributed to an open-source project, and completed the MIT 6.006 Introduction to Algorithms course online. My A-level subjects — Mathematics, Further Mathematics, and Computer Science — have given me both the theoretical foundation and practical skills to pursue this discipline seriously. I am particularly interested in the intersection of algorithms and machine learning, a topic I plan to explore during my undergraduate years.

label: 正常文书 - 经济
essay_text: Reading Ha-Joon Chang's 23 Things They Don't Tell You About Capitalism at age sixteen shattered my naive assumptions about markets and sparked a deeper curiosity about economic systems. Since then, I have read extensively in development economics and monetary theory, and I follow the Bank of England's quarterly inflation reports. At school, I achieved A* in Economics and Mathematics. I led my school's investment club, managing a simulated portfolio and hosting weekly discussions on macroeconomic events. I see economics not as a finished science but as an evolving conversation about how societies organize resources — one I want to join.
